summary: Iteration 3 motion-layer enhancements verified end-to-end. Backend pytest: 10/10 pass (status + leads CRUD untouched and healthy). Frontend desktop (1920x1080): liquid-bg renders with 3 .liquid-blob elements all running CSS keyframes blob-drift-1/2/3, .grid-texture div present inside #liquid-bg, computed pointer-events:none and z-index:-50 confirmed; custom-cursor-dot + custom-cursor-ring both mount, html.cursor-custom-on class added, computed body/html cursor:'none' confirmed; magnetic hooks fire on hover for hero-calendly-cta, hero-jotform-cta, navbar-calendly-cta, final-calendly-cta, final-jotform-cta — each receives inline transform: translate3d(...); 3D tilt confirmed on deliverable-04 (perspective(900px) rotateX rotateY scale) and roadmap-week cards on hover. All v1+v2 features intact: 4 hero stats, 12 deliverables, 4 roadmap weeks, 8 FAQs (2 expand cleanly), naixol IG link, Calendly iframe lazy-loads with shafiqhassan429/mhs URL, footer email + IG links, mobile menu opens with 6 mobile-nav-* links + mobile-calendly-cta. Lead form submission succeeded (POST /api/leads), success block 'Got it. Talk soon.' rendered, DB row written, email_status='skipped' (Resend disabled per design). Reduced-motion gating verified: with prefers-reduced-motion=reduce, liquid-blob count=0, custom-cursor refs not mounted, cursor-custom-on class absent. Zero console errors across all runs. TEST_ leads + status_checks cleaned up post-test.
critical_code_review_comments: CustomCursor.jsx + LiquidBackground.jsx + useMagnetic + useTilt all correctly gate on window.matchMedia('(hover: none)') AND prefers-reduced-motion — defensive and accessible.
useTilt sets transformStyle='preserve-3d' + will-change='transform' on mount and resets on unmount — clean GPU hint lifecycle.
CSS @media (hover: none), (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) hides .liquid-blob, .custom-cursor-dot, .custom-cursor-ring with display:none !important — proper double-gate (JS + CSS).
Magnetic hook resets el.style.transform='' when settled to avoid stuck offsets — good.

Minor: Offer.jsx DeliverableCard uses framer-motion-style entrance (opacity:0 + translateY) which briefly competes with useTilt's transform until the entrance completes; in practice the tilt only activates on hover after viewport reveal so no visible glitch was observed.
Minor: CustomCursor mouseover handler uses e.target.closest(...) on every event — fine at low cost but could be debounced if profiled; not a blocker.
Backend untouched in iteration 3 — server.py /api/leads & /api/status still pass all 10 pytest assertions including _id exclusion, sort desc, EmailStr 422, email_status='skipped'.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_ lead via UI form submission (TEST_iter3_<timestamp>@example.com) + earlier pytest seeded 4 TEST_ leads + 2 TEST_ status_checks during the 10/10 backend run. ALL CLEANED post-test via motor delete_many — leads collection now contains 0 docs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 added motion layer (custom cursor, liquid bg, magnetic CTAs, 3D tilt) on top of iteration_2. Public URL: https://expert-led-courses.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend test files: /app/backend/tests/test_status_api.py + test_leads_api.py (10 tests, all pass). Resend email is intentionally MOCKED (RESEND_API_KEY=placeholder → email_status='skipped'); do not flag. NOTE: Playwright's default browser does NOT emulate '(hover: none)' even at 390x844 viewport, so custom-cursor + liquid-blob remain mounted under set_viewport_size alone — to verify the touch-hide path use page.emulate_media(reduced_motion='reduce') OR a real device emulation context (browser.new_context with is_mobile=True, has_touch=True). I verified the gating works via reduced-motion emulation (blobs/cursor correctly absent). LeadForm testids are 'lead-form-name/email/expertise/revenue/message/submit/success/error' (NOT 'lead-name'). Mobile nav link testid prefix is 'mobile-nav-*' (NOT 'navbar-mobile-link-*').
